mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the effective federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month decreases in FEDFUNDS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls decline by more than 100 thousand persons in a single month, the shock to aggregate income and consumption expectations triggers rapid risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following triggers of PAYEMS declining more than 100 thousand persons, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in the federal funds rate and retail sales indicate that monetary easing is insufficient to offset collapsing consumer demand, and this interaction generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the additive effects of each series alone.
failure_condition: A linear model with the FEDFUNDS-RSAFS interaction term does not outperform a main-effects-only model in predicting 20-day forward returns (delta R-squared < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal binding supply constraints and input cost pressures that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months flagged as late-cycle, INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% indicates a collapse in residential investment and construction employment, transmitting negative wealth effects that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% month-over-month are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirm a recessionary feedback loop between labor income and output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE > 5.0%, falling PAYEMS, and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of qualifying months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month accelerations in consumer price inflation force an upward repricing of the near-term monetary policy path, raising real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL accelerations above 0.4% month-over-month are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in retail sales confirms durable consumer demand, supporting top-line revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth months are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.
